mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 payroll level at 133,009 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains at the zero lower bound, the Fed signals commitment to extended monetary accommodation. This regime produces negative equity risk premia compression and positive forward returns as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.16%, at zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime post-financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over the 2012-2014 period.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand expansion across consumer and construction sectors. This interaction effect amplifies expectations of GDP growth and corporate profit expansion, producing positive 20-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 RSAFS at 404,596 million dollars, HOUST at 717 thousand units
regime_hint: early-cycle demand recovery with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: Joint positive months in RSAFS and HOUST produce negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and forward economic weakness. This triggers negative equity returns as investors reprice earnings expectations downward over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO declines > 0.5% produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling deterioration in labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations in anticipation of demand weakness.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: 3-month UNRATE increases > 0.3pp produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 0.50%, creating a negative real interest rate regime. This interaction signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, reducing equity discount rates and producing positive 60-day forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation backdrop
failure_condition: Months with negative real rates (CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.50%) produce negative 60-day returns in > 48% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion. This always-on signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to confirmed demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2012 GDPC1 at 13,491.0 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized produce negative 60-day returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized contraction across production and labor demand. This interaction effect indicates broad-based economic weakness and triggers negative 20-day equity returns as investors reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2012 INDPRO at 97.2853, PAYEMS at 133,009 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction indicating recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Months with simultaneous INDPRO and PAYEMS declines produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.
